sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Key finding! When I clicked preview box 4, the left V buttons changed from all OFF to all ON:
- Before: ggBggggBggggBggggBgg (all B = OFF)
- After: ggwggggwggggwggggwgg (all w = ON)
And the charcoal shape in the left panel moved! Looking at the image, the charcoal shape is now at the bottom of the left panel instead of the top.
So the preview boxes control:
1. The position of the charcoal shape in the left panel
2. The state of the left V buttons
Preview box 1: yellow square at bottom → charcoal at top, V buttons OFF
Preview box 4: yellow dots at middle → charcoal at bottom, V buttons ON
Now I need to figure out the right configuration. Let me also check the right panel buttons. Currently:
- Right H row 33: WwwwWWwwwWWwwwWWwwwW (all ON)
- Right V row 47: WWBWWWWBWWWWBWWWWBWW (all OFF)
In Level 2, the solution was: turn OFF all right V buttons, press trigger.
In Level 3, the right V buttons are already OFF. But the trigger doesn't work.
Maybe I need to also configure the right H buttons? Or maybe I need a specific preview box selection?
Let me try: with preview box 4 selected (charcoal at bottom, V buttons ON), turn OFF all right H buttons and press trigger.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current state: preview box 1 selected, left V all ON, right V all OFF, right H all ON
# Let me try: select preview box 4, turn OFF right H, press trigger

# First select preview box 4
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 57, 'col': 35}])

# Turn OFF right H buttons (cols 39, 44, 49, 54 at row 33)
for col in [39, 44, 49, 54]:
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 33, 'col': col}])

lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print(f"Left V row 47: {lines[47][6:26]}")
print(f"Right H row 33: {lines[33][37:57]}")
print(f"Right V row 47: {lines[47][37:57]}")

# Press trigger
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 58, 'col': 46}])
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")
print(f"Gameplay changed: {last_action_result.get('gameplay_changed', False)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
